title: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern Are Dead
author: Tom Stoppard
subjects: British and irish drama (dramatic works by one author)
description: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern Are Dead is an absurdist, existential tragicomedy by Tom Stoppard, first staged at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe in 1966. The play expands upon the exploits of two minor characters from Shakespeare's Hamlet, the courtiers Rosencrantz and Guildenstern, and the main setting is Denmark.
